title: prosartes viridescens
intro: Prosartes viridescens, also known as the green adder's mouth, is a perennial wildflower in the lily family native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Prosartes viridescens are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the roots or leaves in hot water.
